description: Operator-facing contract for the pressure-control evidence lane. It records what the runtime pressure snapshot, opt-in admission policy, region memory-budget pressure rows, RCH proof-lane pressure rows, deterministic lab scenario evidence, and pressure triage runbook prove, what they deliberately do not prove, and which RCH-routed command verifies the artifact/docs/source alignment.

proof_lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_runtime_pressure_control_evidence_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test runtime_pressure_control_evidence_contract -- --nocapture

proves: RuntimePressureSnapshot exposes a stable operator-facing schema version and deterministic aggregate classification fields.
Resource, scheduler, spectral, and platform probe signals are folded through the documented snapshot status model.
does_not_prove: A pressure snapshot alone does not prove real-host throughput, optimal scheduling, deadlock, or safe autonomous admission control.

proves: RuntimePressureLabScenarioEvidence covers deterministic classification behavior for the required scenario families under fixed seeds.
Stable JSON projection and fixed-input reproducibility are checked by unit tests in the runtime resource monitor module.
does_not_prove: Lab scenarios do not prove live host capacity, external service performance, or scheduler behavior outside their synthetic inputs.

proves: RuntimePressureAdmissionPolicy is disabled by default and emits deterministic RuntimePressureAdmissionDecision rows when explicitly enabled.
Required work remains admitted so cleanup, cancellation, finalizers, and region quiescence are not blocked by optional admission control.
Optional work fails closed for unknown pressure snapshot schemas.
does_not_prove: The opt-in admission policy does not prove production throughput, autonomous scheduler rewrites, or production-on-by-default admission control.

proves: Fragmented or critical spectral topology can require trapped-cycle investigation before escalation claims are made.
does_not_prove: Spectral fragmentation without explicit trapped-cycle proof is not a deadlock proof.
requires_additional_proof_for: operator escalations that cite proven trapped cycles

proves: RuntimePressureRchProofLaneSnapshot folds externally captured RCH worker admission receipts into redacted deterministic pressure rows.
Remote-required proof-lane refusals preserve local-fallback refusal as critical advisory pressure without probing RCH from the core runtime.
does_not_prove: RCH proof-lane pressure rows do not prove real-host throughput, worker fleet availability, production admission control, or automatic proof-lane scheduling.

proves: RuntimePressureRegionMemoryBudgetSnapshot folds explicitly declared region memory envelopes into deterministic advisory pressure rows.
Region memory-budget overrun evidence can make optional work rejectable through the opt-in admission policy while required cleanup, cancellation, finalizers, and quiescence work remain admitted.
does_not_prove: Region memory-budget pressure rows do not prove per-region allocator accounting, automatic allocation throttling, production throughput, or production-on-by-default admission control.
